schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

identity.canonical_title: Hybrid π-Modulated Sphere Packing and Double-Torus Functor

theory.canonical_statement: To make the DAANSphere discretization explicit, we introduce a hybrid $\pi$-modulated packing functor \begin{equation} \Omega_{\mathrm{pack}} : \mathbf{PackSphere} \longrightarrow \mathbf{TorPack}, \end{equation} sending packing configurations on $S^3$ to toroidal entanglement objects. At the source, objects are packing spheres with local kissing numbers $k(s_i)$ and morphisms generated by embeddings \begin{equation} \mathrm{Hom}\!\bigl(\text{PackingSphere}, S^3\bigr) \ni \Phi : k(s_i) \mapsto d_i(\pi), \end{equation} where $d_i(\pi)$ is the $i$-th decimal digit of $\pi$. The target category carries inner and outer tori $T^2_{\mathrm{in}}$, $T^2_{\mathrm{out}}$ together with a static bubble object and entanglement morphisms \begin{equation} \mathrm{EntMap} : T^2_{\mathrm{in}}(\theta_1^\ast,\phi_1^\ast) \otimes T^2_{\mathrm{out}}(\theta_2^\ast,\phi_2^\ast) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Bubble}_{\mathrm{static}}, \end{equation} realizing directional entanglement across the double torus.
theory.technical_interpretation: Hybrid π-Modulated Sphere Packing and Double-Torus Functor is a symbolic expression, written Hom l(PackingSphere, S^3 r) ni Φ : k(s_i) mapsto d_i(π). It introduces a symbol or relation used elsewhere in the framework. It is built from Φ, π, S, k, s_i, d_i. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formation.inputs: Φ, π, S, k, s_i, d_i
